title: Hungarian Language Basics
excerpt: An introduction to one of Europe's most unique languages — Finno-Ugric, agglutinative, with vowel harmony

children.title: 1. Why Hungarian is Special
children.excerpt: Finno-Ugric origin, agglutinative structure, vowel harmony system

children.title: Finno-Ugric Origin
children.excerpt: Related to Finnish and Estonian, NOT neighboring Slavic or Germanic languages

children.children: Hungarian (magyar) is spoken by ~13 million people and is one of the few non-Indo-European languages in Europe.

The Magyar tribes migrated from the Ural Mountains region around 895 AD, settling in the Carpathian Basin. Despite 1000+ years surrounded by Indo-European languages, Hungarian kept its unique structure.

children.title: Agglutinative Structure
children.excerpt: Words built by stacking suffixes — one word can be a whole sentence!

children.children: Hungarian builds complex meanings by adding suffixes to root words:
ház → házam → házamban → házaimban (house → my house → in my house → in my houses)

children.title: 2. Alphabet & Pronunciation
children.excerpt: 44 letters including 14 vowels and unique digraphs

children.title: Key Digraphs
children.excerpt: sz, cs, gy, ny, zs, ly — two letters representing single sounds

children.children: Hungarian has unique two-letter combinations (digraphs) that count as single letters:

children.title: Vowel Length
children.excerpt: Accent marks indicate LONG vowels, not stress — this changes meaning!

children.children: The accent mark (´) indicates a LONG vowel, not stress. This changes meaning!
Examples: kor (age) vs kór (disease), hal (fish) vs hál (sleeps over)

children.title: 3. Vowel Harmony
children.excerpt: Suffixes must match the vowel type of the root word

children.title: Back vs Front Vowels
children.excerpt: Back: a, á, o, ó, u, ú — Front: e, é, ö, ő, ü, ű

children.children: Hungarian vowels are divided into two groups:
Back vowels: a, á, o, ó, u, ú
Front vowels: e, é, ö, ő, ü, ű

children.title: Suffix Matching
children.excerpt: házban (back) vs kertben (front) — same meaning, different vowels

children.children: Rule: Suffixes must match the vowel type of the root word!
The suffix 'in' (-ban/-ben) changes based on the root:
ház (house, back vowel) → házban (in the house)
kert (garden, front vowel) → kertben (in the garden)

children.title: 4. Essential Phrases
children.excerpt: Greetings, yes/no, thank you, and more

children.title: 5. Agglutination Demo
children.excerpt: ház → házam → házamban — watch a word grow!

children.children: Watch how suffixes stack to build meaning:

children.title: 6. Numbers 1-10
children.excerpt: egy, kettő, három... Learn to count in Hungarian

children.children: Fun fact: Hungarian uses 'kettő' for standalone '2' but 'két' before nouns (két alma = two apples).
